title: Click Save to create the federated database instance with virtual databases, collections, and views mapped to your online archive.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
Before you begin, you will need:

- An Atlas online archive in the same project where you intend to
  create the federated database instance.

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
- Click the Create New Federated Database dropdown.

- Select Manual Setup.You can select AWS (Amazon Web Services), Azure (Microsoft Azure), or Google Cloud. Once your federated database instance is created, you
can't change the cloud provider where Atlas Data Federation processes your queries.We recommend that you select the same cloud provider as the cloud provider that is hosting your data.Defaults to `FederatedDatabaseInstance[n]`. Once your federated database instance is
created, you can't change its name.- For a guided experience, click Visual Editor.

- To edit the raw JSON (Javascript Object Notation), click JSON Editor.<Tab tabid="visual">
  Visual Editor

  - Select Atlas Online Archive from the Data Sources
    dropdown to configure a federated database instance for archived data.

    Corresponds to [stores.\[n\].provider](datalakeconf-adl.stores.\[n].provider)
    JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

  - (Optional) Click the  for the:

    - Database to edit the database name. Defaults to
      `VirtualDatabase[n]`.

      Corresponds to [databases.\[n\].name](datalakeconf-adl.databases.\[n].name)
      JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

    - Collection to edit the collection name. Defaults to
      `VirtualCollection[n]`.

      Corresponds to [databases.\[n\].collections.\[n\].name](datalakeconf-adl.databases.\[n].collections.\[n].name)
      JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

    - View to edit the view name.

    You can click:

    - Add Database to add databases and
      collections.

    -  associated with the database to
      add collections to the database.

    -  associated with the collection to
      add [views](manual/core/views) on the
      collection. To create a view, you must specify:

      - The name of the view.

      - The [pipeline](manual/core/aggregation-pipeline)
        to apply to the view.

        > **Note:** The view definition pipeline can't include the
        > `$out` or the `$merge` stage. If the view
        > definition includes nested pipeline stages such
        > as `$lookup` or `$facet`, this restriction
        > applies to those nested pipelines as well.

        To learn more about views, see:

        - [Views](manual/core/views)

        - [db.createView](manual/reference/method/db.createView)

      -  associated with the database,
        collection, or view to remove it.

  - Drag and drop the Atlas Online Archive to map
    with the collection.

    Corresponds to [databases.\[n\].collections.\[n\].dataSources](datalakeconf-adl.databases.\[n].collections.\[n].dataSources)
    JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.
</Tab>JSON EditorYour configuration for online archive should look similar to the following:```json
{
  "stores" : [
    {
      "name" : "<string>",
      "provider": "<string>",
      "region": "<string>"
    }
  ],
  "databases" : [
      {
        "name" : "<string>",
        "collections" : [
          {
            "name" : "<string>",
            "dataSources" : [
              {
                "storeName" : "<string>",
                "datasetName" : "<string>",
                "datasetPrefix": "<string>",
                "trimLevel": <int>,
                "provenanceFieldName": "<string>",
                "maxDatasets": <int>
              }
            ]
          }
        ],
        "views" : [ 
          {
            "name" : "<string>", 
            "source" : "<string>", 
            "pipeline" : "<string>" 
          }
        ] 
      }
  ]
}
  
```To learn more about these configuration settings, see
[Online Archives](adf-configuration-file-adl).- Define your dataset or online archive as a data store in
  your federated database instance storage configuration.

  Edit the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration settings shown in the UI
  for `stores`. Your `stores` cofiguration setting
  should resemble the following:

  ```json
  "stores" : [
    {
      "name" : "<string>",
      "provider": "<string>",
      "region": "<string>"
    }
  ]

  ```

- Define virtual databases, collections, and views for
  your dataset or online archive in your federated database instance storage
  configuration.

  ```json
  "databases" : [
    {
      "name" : "<string>",
      "collections" : [
        {
          "name" : "<string>",
          "dataSources" : [
            {
              "storeName" : "<string>",
              "datasetName" : "<string>",
              "datasetPrefix": "<string>",
              "trimLevel": <int>,
              "provenanceFieldName": "<string>",
              "maxDatasets": <int>
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "views" : [ 
        {
          "name" : "<string>", 
          "source" : "<string>", 
          "pipeline" : "<string>" 
        }
      ] 
    }
  ]

  ```To add other data stores for federated queries, see:- [Create AWS Data Store From the UI](deploy-s3) or
  [Create Azure Data Store From the UI](deploy-azure)

- [Create HTTP or HTTPS Data Store From the UI](deploy-http-datastore)

- [Create Atlas Data Store From the UI](deploy-atlas-datastore)
